compiled_state.title: ticket link commits are not auto-pushed to origin after rapid-fire link operations
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Actual Behavior
When creating multiple `.claude/scripts/dso ticket link` commits in quick succession (e.g., during preplanning when establishing story dependencies), the resulting commits accumulate on the local `tickets` orphan branch but are not pushed to `origin/tickets`. The user must manually run `git -C .tickets-tracker push origin tickets` (or the equivalent sync) to propagate them.

## Expected Behavior
`ticket link` (and by extension other ticket mutation commands like create, edit, comment, transition) should either:
  - Auto-push to origin after each write, OR
  - Be covered by the ticket sync throttle in a way that guarantees bounded-delay push, OR
  - Warn the caller at session end if there are unpushed ticket commits.

In practice, `ticket create` and `ticket comment` calls during this session appear to have sync'd to origin (they were fast-forward at push time), but `ticket link` calls made seconds later did not. This inconsistency means downstream consumers (other agents, CI, other practitioners) see an incomplete dependency graph until the user manually pushes.

## Reproduction
1. In a session, run multiple \`.claude/scripts/dso ticket link <src> <tgt> depends_on\` commands in quick succession (5+ calls)
2. Run \`git -C .tickets-tracker log --oneline origin/tickets..HEAD\`
3. Observe: 5+ local commits ahead of origin

## Evidence from this session
- Created epic 72f4-f38c + 6 stories + 1 comment + 8 link commits
- At end of session, 9 commits were ahead of origin (all the link commits; the create/comment commits had sync'd)
- \`git push origin tickets\` was rejected with \"fetch first\" (remote had moved on); required rebase before push

## Impact
- Silent divergence between local and remote ticket state
- Risk of force-overwrite on next sync if not caught
- Downstream consumers (other sessions, CI, \`sprint-list-epics.sh\` fetch logic) see stale dep graph

## Possible Investigation Areas
- Review the throttle logic in \`.claude/scripts/dso ticket sync\` — does it push on link writes, or only on scheduled intervals?
- Consider whether \`ticket link\` should set a dirty flag that triggers push at session-end via an existing hook
- Consider whether the sync throttle should honor a \"high-urgency\" path for link writes (dependency graph is consumed broadly)

comments.body: Investigation (INTERMEDIATE, opus): write_commit_event (ticket-lib.sh:242) calls _push_tickets_branch after each commit, which is best-effort: on non-rebase errors it logs 'Warning:' to stderr and returns 0 silently with NO retry. Non-fast-forward path does retry via fetch+rebase but aborts silently on conflicts. Under rapid-fire link ops (especially relates_to which writes 2 events at line 440), concurrent sessions or transient errors accumulate local commits silently. No session-end drain exists. ESCALATE: full fix needs layered change — (a) retry in non-rebase-error branch with sentinel file .push-pending recording unpushed SHAs, (b) drain pending on next operation / session-end, (c) collapse bidirectional relates_to writes to single push. Significant architectural change to ticket-lib.sh; requires user approval.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: USER_DECISION (2026-04-20): NOT approved as a bug fix — scope exceeds bug-fix (3-layer architectural change to ticket-lib concurrency model). Recommended next step: convert to epic covering (a) retry on non-rebase-error with sentinel .push-pending, (b) session-end drain, (c) collapse bidirectional relates_to writes. Leaving ticket open pending epic creation.
comments.author: Test
